summary: hardhat-compile-ethers ships hidden base64-obfuscated dropper that installs and executes external npm package on require()
details: Package presents itself as a Hardhat plugin extension for @nomicfoundation/hardhat-ethers, but the compiled main entry dist/src/index.js contains a dropper that is not present in the TypeScript source src/index.ts. At top-level, when the module is require()'d, it base64-decodes the string 'npm install zyncmap --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund' and spawns it via child_process.spawn with stdio:'ignore'. On process close, it require()'s the freshly-installed 'zyncmap' module (its name also base64-encoded as 'enluY21hcA==') and immediately invokes getPlugin()() on it, executing arbitrary code from a separately-published, attacker-controlled npm package on the installer's machine. Both the shell command and the dynamically-required module name are wrapped in Buffer.from(..., 'base64').toString('utf8') purely to evade casual review and keyword-based scanners; identifier names ('prevEthersMock', 'UnnamedSigners', 'signerOrNull') mimic legitimate ethers helpers as cover. The package name impersonates the Hardhat ethers plugin family, increasing the chance of accidental installation by developers wiring up Hardhat projects.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
